blocks.task: Profile HenryDB: which queries are slowest? Where are the bottlenecks?
blocks.summary: Profiled HenryDB with 5000 rows: reads fast (10-26ms), writes slow (2ms/insert with index). Full scan 4.2μs/row, GROUP BY 14.5ms, Window fn 23.9ms over 5K rows. Analyzes queries for column access patterns (filters, joins, ORDER BY, GROUP BY), detects composite index opportunities, deduplicates against existing indexes, estimates improvement level, generates CREATE INDEX SQL. PostgreSQL pg_qualstats inspired.

blocks.task: Study neural-net RoPE implementation — rotary position embeddings
blocks.summary: RoPE (181 LOC): frequency basis θ_i = 1/(10000^(2i/d)), 2D rotation per dimension pair. Key property: dot product depends only on relative position (m-n). Backward via inverse rotation (orthogonal). Position offset for KV cache. NTK-aware scaling + YaRN for extrapolation beyond training length.

blocks.task: Study HenryDB Grace hash join — for large datasets
blocks.summary: Grace hash join (103 LOC): 2-phase partitioned hash join for bounded memory. Phase 1: hash-partition both inputs. Phase 2: build+probe hash table per partition pair. Named after Grace database machine (Kitsuregawa 1983). With performance stats tracking.
blocks.details: Grace hash join (103 LOC): 2-phase partitioned hash join for bounded memory. Phase 1: hash-partition both inputs. Phase 2: build+probe hash table per partition pair. Named after Grace database machine (Kitsuregawa 1983). With performance stats tracking.

blocks.task: Study neural-net autograd — automatic differentiation engine
blocks.summary: Autograd (233 LOC): PyTorch-style reverse-mode AD. Variable class with value/grad/children/backward closure. Topological sort for reverse traversal. 15+ ops: add/mul/sub/div/pow/neg/exp/log/sin/cos/tanh/sigmoid/relu/softplus/abs. Each op stores backward as closure capturing inputs.

blocks.task: Study HenryDB cuckoo filter — probabilistic set membership
blocks.summary: Cuckoo filter (167 LOC): fingerprint-based set membership with deletion support. Dual indices via XOR trick (i2 = i1 ⊕ hash(fp)), 4-entry buckets, 500-kick eviction limit. Better than Bloom for FPR < 3%. Correct Fan et al. 2014 implementation.

blocks.task: Study neural-net GNN (graph neural network) — message passing
blocks.summary: GNN (252 LOC): Graph class (adjacency list + node features), GCNLayer (mean neighbor aggregation → linear → activation, Kipf & Welling 2017), GCN network (multi-layer, node classification + graph-level prediction via global mean pool). Karate Club demo included.

blocks.task: Study HenryDB join executor — the 1078 LOC join engine
blocks.summary: HenryDB join executor (1078 LOC): nested loop, hash join (with index pushdown), merge join. All join types: INNER/LEFT/RIGHT/FULL/CROSS/NATURAL. Cost estimation (scan vs index, join algorithm selection). Join reordering via dynamic programming on subset lattice. NDV estimation for join selectivity.

blocks.task: Study neural-net differentiable sorting — Sinkhorn
blocks.summary: Differentiable sorting (134 LOC): softSort (optimal transport via Sinkhorn → doubly-stochastic permutation matrix), sinkhorn (alternating row/col normalization), softRank (sigmoid-approximated argsort), softTopK (differentiable element selection), neuralSort (Grover et al. stochastic sorting). Complete differentiable sorting toolkit.

blocks.task: Study neural-net predictive coding — hierarchical prediction error minimization
blocks.summary: Predictive coding (257 LOC): hierarchical prediction error minimization (Rao & Ballard 1999). Each layer: value nodes μ, error nodes ε, generative weights W, precision Π. Top-down predictions, bottom-up errors. LOCAL learning only — no backprop needed! Connected to Free Energy Principle (Friston 2005). Separate inference and learning phases.

blocks.task: Study HenryDB SipHash — cryptographic hash for security-sensitive contexts
blocks.summary: SipHash (29 LOC): SipHash-2-4 with BigInt arithmetic. 4 state variables, rotl mixing, 2 compression rounds per 8-byte block, 4 finalization rounds. DoS-resistant hash for hash tables (Python dict, Rust HashMap, Redis). Compact and correct.

blocks.task: Study neural-net BPE tokenizer implementation
blocks.summary: BPE tokenizer (211 LOC): train (char-level → iterative pair merging → vocab), encode (chars → apply merges), decode (token ID → string). Special tokens support, whitespace-aware word splitting, word count stats. Correct Sennrich et al. 2016 implementation.

blocks.task: Study HenryDB skip list — MVCC-aware concurrent data structure
blocks.summary: Skip list (174 LOC): MAX_LEVEL 32, P=0.25 (Redis-style). O(log n) insert/search/delete with probabilistic levels. Range scan via forward pointers. Custom comparator support. Used in Redis sorted sets, LevelDB/RocksDB memtable.

blocks.task: Study neural-net capsule network — dynamic routing between capsules
blocks.summary: Capsule networks (345 LOC): squash activation (direction-preserving, length → [0,1]), full backward gradient. CapsuleLayer with dynamic routing: transformation matrices W[i][j], iterative coupling coefficient updates, agreement-based routing. Margin loss for classification. Correct Sabour, Frosst, Hinton 2017.

blocks.task: Study HenryDB Zobrist hashing — for transposition tables
blocks.summary: Zobrist hashing (84 LOC): random 64-bit BigInt table (positions × values), O(1) incremental XOR updates (XOR is self-inverse). Set variant with lazy hash table. Used in chess engines, DB deduplication, change detection.

blocks.task: Study neural-net VAE — variational autoencoder with reparameterization
blocks.summary: VAE (244 LOC): encoder (x → μ, logσ²), reparameterization trick (z = μ + σ·ε), decoder (z → x̂). ELBO loss = reconstruction + β·KL. β-VAE support (Higgins 2017). Full training loop with backward pass through reparameterization. Correct Kingma & Welling 2014.

blocks.task: Study HenryDB perfect hashing — minimal perfect hash function
blocks.summary: Perfect hashing (58 LOC): two-level scheme. Level 1: distribute keys into buckets by h(key,0). Level 2: find per-bucket seed that maps all keys to unique slots. O(N) build, O(1) lookup, zero collisions. Only for static key sets.

blocks.task: Study neural-net contrastive learning — SimCLR
blocks.summary: SimCLR contrastive learning (167 LOC): NT-Xent loss with cosine similarity / temperature. Positive pairs (z[2i], z[2i+1]) = augmented views. 2N×2N similarity matrix, numerically stable log-sum-exp, accuracy tracking. Data augmentation pipeline (noise, mask, scale). Correct Chen et al. 2020.

blocks.task: Study neural-net GRU — gated recurrent unit
